sources_exist.note: Six of seven canonical citations resolved and the four deterministic dataset inputs exactly match the supplied dataset, but the Contract Pharma pricing citation returned HTTP 402 and could not be audited.

figures_match_sources.note: The accessible pages contain broad pharma adoption, clinical-research-site multiples, CRO M&A examples, and generic small-business demographics, but none supports the score-bearing 541714 value attributed to it; the pricing page was inaccessible.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: The bounded task fractions and physical-lab moat are directionally plausible, but pi_dist=0.40 depends on an unauditable 50% FTE revenue share and t50=5 depends on NGS, bioinformatics, and LIMS adoption timelines absent from the cited page. The record also does not establish a dominant, acquirable preclinical CRO subsegment.

rubric_consistent.note: Clinical-research-site valuation ranges are applied to preclinical CRO and biotechnology R&D businesses without a demonstrated business-model normalization, while several unsupported inputs retain MED quality and materially affect A, B, C, and M. ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on B, M, S is within the stated tolerance and is not independently disqualifying.

cross_checks_honest.note: The record acknowledges service-model heterogeneity, but the pricing cross-check repeats an unaudited 50%/35%/15% mix and the adoption cross-check relies on survey figures that neither establish CRO production use nor all of the task-level percentages quoted.

source_audits.note: The page documents broad clinical-CRO consolidation and selected transactions, but it does not substantiate the record's exact five active PE-backed preclinical or biotech CRO platforms; some listed names are absent and the cited activity spans clinical and other service tiers.

The page reports 4-6x for standalone clinical research sites below $5 million EBITDA and 7-9x for sites at $5-10 million EBITDA. It addresses clinical research site operators, not preclinical CRO or 541714 biotechnology R&D targets, and the record supplies no comparability adjustment supporting a 7.0x buy multiple.

The page reports broad pharma/biotech survey measures including 49% use in R&D, 13% comprehensive implementation, and 92% with some AI exposure. It does not establish a 35% material production-use rate for fee-for-service CROs, and the cited 76%, 71%, and 66% task figures are not all present.

The page reports a 17.8x public-CRO median and low-teens valuation for larger clinical research site networks with at least $10 million EBITDA. Those are different populations from a preclinical CRO platform, and the record does not demonstrate that 13.0x is an in-scope normalized exit multiple.

The article discusses an AI power shift in drug discovery but provides no dated NGS or bioinformatics adoption series, no LIMS adoption timeline, and no evidence for a seven-year historical analog or five-year time to 50% material AI use.

The generic marketing blog reports 50.9% of small-business owners ages 50-88 and 40-41% Boomer ownership. It does not report an age-60+ share, a 37% share ages 55-73, or CRO founder and principal demographics supporting the estimated 0.28.

The cited page returned HTTP 402 on repeated access and was not auditable. Consequently it cannot validate the asserted 50% FTE, 35% fee-for-service or unit-priced, and 15% milestone revenue mix or the claimed 60% FTE real-rate decline.

reasons: inputs.historical_analogs at https://www.drugdiscoverynews.com/the-2026-ai-power-shift-17020 contains no NGS, bioinformatics, or LIMS adoption timeline, so inputs.t50_years and score A lack a valid chain; rerun with a dated, comparable adoption series or a fully documented ESTIMATE.

inputs.pricing_structure at https://www.contractpharma.com/full-time-equivalent-agreements/ is inaccessible and cannot support the 50%/35%/15% revenue mix or real-rate decline used by inputs.pi_dist and score C; rerun with an accessible 541714 pricing-mix source and an auditable capture calculation.

inputs.buy_mult and inputs.exit_mult at https://www.objectiveibv.com/resources/life-sciences-tech/valuation-of-clinical-research-sites-what-buyers-look-for-in-a-clinical-research-site-company/ use clinical research site and public-CRO benchmarks without normalizing them to preclinical CRO or biotechnology R&D targets; rerun with in-scope transactions or an explicit service-model and size adjustment.

inputs.active_consolidators at https://intuitionlabs.ai/articles/cro-consolidation-clinical-trials-impact does not verify the exact five named active preclinical or biotech CRO platforms, so score B lacks a reproducible count; rerun with dated, in-scope platform ownership and add-on evidence.

inputs.current_adoption_pct at https://intuitionlabs.ai/articles/ai-adoption-pharma-biotech-benchmarks converts broad pharma and biotech survey data into a 35% CRO production-use estimate without evidence, while inputs.owners_60plus_pct at https://www.digitalwebsolutions.com/blog/average-age-of-small-business-owners/ supplies no CRO age-60+ statistic; rerun both with directly comparable data or transparent ESTIMATE derivations.
flags_reviewed: ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on B, M, S
